name: ar9271-sovereign-build

documentation.readme: Reproducible recipe for building signed custom firmware for Qualcomm Atheros AR9271 USB WiFi dongles (TP-Link TL-WN722Nv1, Alfa AWUS036NHA, ASUS USB-N13 v1, and others), validated on the live chip with automatic rollback.
usage_sections: Reproducible cross-compiler: xtensa-elf-gcc 10.2.0 targeting AR9271's specific Xtensa core (XCHALHAVEDIV32=0, MUL32HIGH=1, ABSOLUTELITERALS=1)
A signed htc9271.fw binary (51008 bytes — identical slot to vendor)
Live-test confirmation via dmesg that the chip enumerates with your firmware
A clean rollback path so production WiFi isn't at risk
Not a LED/retry mod. Those policies live in the kernel driver (ath9khtc), not in the firmware. The firmware is a thin layer: USB protocol + low-level MAC + WMI service.

Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with sokpheng001/Wifi-exploit-attack-in-KH (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: ar9271-sovereign-build.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Reproducible recipe for building signed custom firmware for Qualcomm Atheros AR9271 USB WiFi dongles (TP-Link TL-WN722Nv1, Alfa AWUS036NHA, ASUS USB-N13 v1, and others), validated on the live chip with automatic rollback.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/qca/open-ath9k-htc-firmware.git ~/src/ath9k-htc-firmware; docker run --rm -v "$PWD":/work -w /work debian:11 bash -c ". Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `make: *** [Makefile:147: .../gmp-6.2.0/.built] Błąd 1`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Wireless, use ar9271-sovereign-build because ar9271-sovereign-build.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `make: *** [Makefile:147: .../gmp-6.2.0/.built] Błąd 1` as documented upstream for ar9271-sovereign-build.
When chaining Wireless tools, pair ar9271-sovereign-build with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ar9271-sovereign-build --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
